id: c_plainContext_v1_EARLY_L40_Excessive phone or social media use
scriptId: cs_v1_EARLY_L40_Excessive phone or social media use
historyId: h_v1_EARLY_L40_Excessive phone or social media use
context: Active facts/constraints: There are healthful gamified apps that reward mindful use rather than exploiting attention; healthy-app criteria include clear goals, rewards for mindful behavior (no variable surprises), no autoplay/feeds, transparent permissions/privacy, and easy integration with OS controls; documented examples with notes: Forest (iOS/Android/Chrome extension) core concept: plant a virtual tree that grows while off phone; rewards via coins toward trees or real-tree donations; healthful because it rewards not using the device; caveat: notifications may require additional controls; Flipd (iOS/Android) core: focus sessions that lock the phone or hide distracting apps; supports group challenges; healthful due to enforced sessions and social accountability without in-feed browsing; caveat: full lock may require elevated permissions; Hold (iOS) core: streaks/achievements for time away; lightweight optional social sharing; healthful due to positive streaks; caveat: social features can create pressure; Habitica (iOS/Android/Web) core: gamified to-do/habit tracker; rewards avatar progression; healthful as you can treat “no social-media during X hours” as a habit; caveat: more task-management than blocking; Focus To-Do / Pomodoro apps (iOS/Android) with streaks/achievements core: pomodoro timers + progress tracking; healthful due to structured focus blocks and small rewards; caveat: not heavy blockers; Space (iOS/Android) / ActionDash (Android) core: coaching + gentle gamification; healthful: promotes reflection and small wins; caveat: not heavily gamified; Browser extensions with light gamification (Forest extension; Habitica with desktop rules) core: apply abstinence/achievement concept to desktop/browsing; rationale: helps prevent cross-device displacement; Privacy/permission notes: blockers/lock apps often request Accessibility or VPN-style permissions; trade-offs: stronger enforcement vs increased access to activity data; prefer apps with minimal data, opt-out of social sharing, clearly documented data usage; How to use: pair a mindful-reward gamified app (Forest, Hold) with OS-level controls (disable notifications, set app limits) to reduce triggers and provide scaffolding; keep rewards meaningful but low-pressure (personal progress stats, small virtual rewards, occasional real-world treat); trial duration: 1–2 weeks to assess impact on pickups and motivation; Optional capability: I can name 2–3 apps that best match a particular preference (strict lock vs gentle nudge vs social accountability).
